title: Insert Uniface Objects in the Server Page Layout
breadcrumbs: Create the Component Layout of Server Pages
Insert Uniface Objects in the Server Page Layout
text: Insert Uniface Objects in the Server Page Layout When creating the layout, elements in the layout must be bound to the component data structure as defined in the Component Editor. You can do this by copying the Uniface objects from the component structure and inserting them as HTML in the layout. The component structure must be defined before creating the layout. For more information, see Define the Component Structure . In the Component Editor, click Design Layout . In the Structure tab, select the objects you want to insert in the layout, then right-click and choose one of the Copy options. Table 1. Copy as HTML Menu Commands Command Description As Horizontal Table Formats all selected entities and their content as a horizontal table, with labels in the first row, and entity occurrence data in the second row. Inner entities are copied as tables within tables. As Unformatted HTML Formats the all selected objects as elements enclosed in <div> tags. As Binding Information Formats only the object type and name as an id or name attribute and its value. For example, id="ufld:BTN_STORE.ORDERS.SALES" The appropriate XHTML is generated and inserted into the layout. The generated XHTML for a Dynamic Server Page contains standards-compliant XHTML. For Static Server Pages, it contains Uniface-specific elements. For more information, see Layout of Static Server Pages . CAUTION: Uniface does not synchronize the component structure and server page layout. If you subsequently make changes in the component structure or to widget properties, you also need to update the layout. Related Links XHTML The Extensible HyperText Markup Language (XHTML) is an application of XML in which a document is presented as a structure of nested elements. Along with content, an XHTML document provides its own logical structure without specifying how it should be rendered. Aspects such as font, size, and indentation are therefore determined by the application that interprets the document. Set Uniface IDE Preferences You can configure the behavior of the Uniface IDE by setting preferences in the IDE assignment and initialization files. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
